body:
Title: Revenu Quebec Schedule K 2025 premium payable under the Quebec prescription drug insurance plan.

Source:
- https://www.revenuquebec.ca/en/online-services/forms-and-publications/current-details/tp-1-v/
- https://www.revenuquebec.ca/documents/en/formulaires/tp/2025-12/TP-1.D.K-V%282025-12%29.pdf

Schedule K numeric source values for encoded amounts, rates, thresholds, caps, and limits:
Value: 12467
Value: 7468
Value: 6892
Value: 11534
Value: 19890
Value: 32240
Value: 4220
Value: 8120
Value: 12350
Value: 16570
Value: 5000
Value: 14669
Value: 766
Value: 755
Value: 62
Value: 62.00
Value: 63.83
Value: 7.84
Value: 0.0784
Value: 11.76
Value: 0.1176
Value: 3.93
Value: 0.0393
Value: 5.89
Value: 0.0589
Value: 392
Value: 392.00
Value: 196.5
Value: 196.50
Value: 12

Schedule K begins with full-year exemption situations for box 449, including group-plan coverage, last-resort financial assistance, a spouse paying the premium, full-year excluded situations from lines 53, 55 or 56, older taxpayers with net federal supplements above the listed limits, no-spouse taxpayers with line 275 of $19,890 or less, and spouse taxpayers with combined line 275 of $32,240 or less.

Part A calculates income used to calculate the premium. Line 40 adds the taxpayer's line 275 income on line 36 and the spouse's line 275 income on line 37. Line 41 uses $32,240 if the taxpayer had a spouse on December 31, 2025, otherwise $19,890. Line 42 adds $4,220 for one dependent child or $8,120 for more than one dependent child when the taxpayer had a spouse. Line 44 adds $12,350 for one dependent child or $16,570 for more than one dependent child when the taxpayer did not have a spouse. Line 46 adds line 41 and the applicable line 42 or line 44 amount. Line 48 subtracts line 46 from line 40 and floors the result at zero.

Part B calculates months for which no premium is payable. Section 1 records the taxpayer's exempt months and line 62 adds boxes 60 and 61. Section 2 records the spouse's exempt months and line 76 adds boxes 74 and 75. The schedule says not to check the same month more than once.

Part C calculates the premium. If line 48 is $5,000 or less, the taxpayer completes column A only; if line 48 is more than $5,000 but not more than $14,669, the taxpayer completes column B only; if line 48 is more than $14,669, line 84 is $766. For an individual without a spouse, column A subtracts $0 and applies 7.84%, while column B subtracts $5,000, applies 11.76%, and adds $392.00. For an individual with a spouse, column A subtracts $0 and applies 3.93%, while column B subtracts $5,000, applies 5.89%, and adds $196.50. Line 83 is capped at $766 and line 84 carries the applicable column amount.

Line 85 multiplies line 84 by the taxpayer's line 62 months and divides by 12. Line 86 subtracts line 85 from line 84. Line 87 gives the 2025 maximum premium as $755. Line 88 adds taxpayer January-to-June exempt months multiplied by $62.00 and July-to-December exempt months multiplied by $63.83. Line 89 subtracts line 88 from line 87. Line 90 enters the lesser of line 86 and line 89. If the taxpayer wishes to pay the spouse's premium, line 92 multiplies line 91 by the spouse's line 76 months and divides by 12, line 93 subtracts line 92 from line 91, line 94 gives the $755 maximum premium, line 95 applies the $62.00 and $63.83 monthly reductions to spouse exempt months, line 96 subtracts line 95 from line 94, and line 97 enters the lesser of line 93 and line 96. Line 98 adds lines 90 and 97 and carries the premium payable to TP-1 line 447.
